title: The City After Dark
subtitle: Order and liberty rarely shake hands.

turns.prompt: Violent crime is surging and the public is afraid to go out at night. They want it fixed now.

choices.label: Flood the streets with police

delayed.headline: Aggressive policing sparks a community backlash

choices.reaction: Patrols on every corner. Some feel safer, some feel watched.

choices.label: Invest in jobs, youth, and housing
choices.flavour: Treat the disease, not the symptom.
choices.reaction: A long-term bet placed. Tonight's victims are unmoved.

choices.label: Pass harsh mandatory sentences
choices.flavour: Make the price of crime brutal.
choices.reaction: Prisons fill. The crime stats dip — for now.

turns.prompt: Crime ticks down. Your approval ticks up. There's room to shape the system.

choices.label: Build community-led policing
choices.flavour: Police with people, not at them.

choices.label: Blanket the city in cameras and data
choices.flavour: If you can see it, you can stop it.

delayed.headline: A data breach exposes everyone the cameras watched

choices.reaction: Eyes everywhere. Crime slips into the shadows.

choices.label: Privatise prisons to cut costs
choices.flavour: Let the market run the cells.
choices.reaction: The budget eases. Conditions quietly worsen.

turns.prompt: A death in police custody is caught on camera. The city erupts in protest.

choices.label: Crack down hard on the protests
choices.reaction: Order restored by force. Resentment hardens into something lasting.

choices.label: Open an independent inquiry
choices.reaction: Cameras roll at the hearings. Justice moves at its own pace.

turns.prompt: The choices you made to look tough are now the story.

memory_callbacks.text: The heavy policing you ordered is fuelling the very unrest on the streets.
The surveillance network has become a scandal all of its own.
The crackdown turned scattered protesters into a national movement.
Conditions in the private prisons have become a public disgrace.

choices.label: Rebuild the justice system from the ground up
choices.reaction: A fairer system, slowly. The backlog runs years deep.

choices.label: Double down on order at any cost
choices.reaction: The streets go quiet. The cost is counted later.

choices.label: Decriminalise low-level offences
choices.reaction: Courts breathe again. Critics warn of disorder.

turns.prompt: The unrest settles. What kind of justice did your society choose?

choices.label: Build a restorative-justice society
choices.flavour: Repair harm, not just punish it.
choices.reaction: Fewer cells, more second chances. Not everyone forgives.

choices.label: Cement a hard security state
choices.reaction: Crime is low. So is freedom.

choices.label: Balance enforcement with opportunity
choices.reaction: A workable peace — imperfect and forever contested.
